decisions.title: Approve FedRAMP compliance sprint scope
decisions.context: 7 security tickets (ENG-9341, 9307, 9326, 9304, 9305, 9694, 9695) cluster around FedRAMP requirements. Completing these unblocks enterprise expansion under 'Build the Moat' pillar.

arguments.for: Clear regulatory requirement for enterprise deals; bundled completion reduces context-switching; aligns with 'Build the Moat' through compliance differentiation
arguments.against: Delays feature velocity on Explore V2 and Canvas; requires specialized security expertise; may introduce breaking changes to user sessions

decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 (E2) GA blockers vs technical debt
decisions.context: E2 has 5 active tickets (ENG-9779, 9981, 9533, 9972, 9989) nearing completion, but Canvas (ENG-9571) and core platform work (Flink 2.0, Horizons Integration) compete for resources. E2 is core to 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: E2 GA directly increases 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' metric; feature flags audit (ENG-9779) unblocks production release; customer-facing impact highest
arguments.against: Technical debt (Flink 2.0, security groups) accumulates interest; Canvas rework (ENG-9571) affects user retention; infrastructure stability risks grow

decisions.title: Decide Redpanda deployment strategy (ENG-9176)
decisions.context: Deploying Redpanda into EC2 represents infrastructure divergence from managed Kafka. Impacts 'Embed Deeper' through data ingestion reliability but introduces operational complexity.
decisions.blockedBy: Cost-benefit analysis incomplete; runbook documentation (ENG-9997) patterns need standardization first

arguments.for: Performance improvements for real-time data flows; cost reduction vs managed Kafka; aligns with Catalog Update Service work (ENG-9424, 9427)
arguments.against: Increases operational burden; diverts resources from FedRAMP work; introduces new failure modes without mature monitoring

decisions.title: Commit to Checkly for E2E testing (ENG-9585)
decisions.context: Playwright/Bullfinch setup (ENG-9972) and cross-browser testing (ENG-8943) signal testing infrastructure investment. Need decision on external monitoring to support 'Nail the Core' reliability.

arguments.for: External validation catches issues before customers; complements Playwright work; vendor support reduces maintenance; aligns with E2 GA quality bar
arguments.against: Adds monthly SaaS cost; overlaps with internal Playwright capability; vendor lock-in risk; team lacks bandwidth for integration

decisions.title: Resolve feature flag failure handling strategy (ENG-9995)
decisions.context: Current behavior returns errors on flag load failure. Decision needed: default to safe behavior vs fail-fast. Critical for 'Nail the Core' stability as E2 reaches GA.

arguments.for: Graceful degradation improves uptime; reduces customer-facing errors; aligns with availability over consistency for feature toggles; unblocks E2 GA confidence
arguments.against: Silent failures mask configuration issues; harder to debug; may serve inconsistent experiences; complicates rollback procedures

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination load from parallel FedRAMP, E2 GA, and infrastructure initiatives competing for eng time
alerts: Only 20% free time this week - strategic decisions blocked by meeting overhead
3 one-on-ones today - consider batching to preserve focus blocks
No maker time Thu/Fri - E2 GA blockers need uninterrupted attention
